title: Digital Realty Launches ServiceFabric® MCP, Bringing AI-Native Programmable Control to 800+ Data Centers
author: Digital Realty
description: Digital Realty announced the availability of ServiceFabric® Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open protocol that makes infrastructure programmable for Private AI environments across its 800+ global data centers. The platform provides AI-native control surfaces for design, provisioning, discovery, security, and operations integration, enabling enterprises to deploy and manage AI infrastructure at scale without vendor lock-in.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company announced a significant new product launch (ServiceFabric MCP) that extends its platform capabilities for enterprise AI deployments across 800+ data centers. The announcement demonstrates technological innovation, strategic positioning in the growing AI infrastructure market, and validation from customers and partners. The open-by-design approach and broad ecosystem partnerships strengthen competitive positioning.

title: Digital Realty Launches Malaysia Operations to Advance Southeast Asia’s Digital Connectivity
author: Na
description: Digital Realty announced the establishment of its Malaysia platform in Cyberjaya, marking a key milestone in its Asia Pacific expansion. The company plans to develop approximately 32 megawatts of data center capacity across three connected facilities (KUL10, KUL11, and a future expansion site), integrating Malaysia into its global PlatformDIGITAL to support AI and data-driven workloads. The inauguration was officiated by Malaysia's Minister of Digital and supported by MIDA and MDEC, reflecting the country's commitment to becoming a regional digital and AI hub.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Digital Realty is expanding its global footprint with a significant new platform in Malaysia, demonstrating strong conviction in the region's growth potential. The rapid establishment of a multi-site presence with 32MW capacity, support from government officials, and integration into its global platform indicate successful market entry and positive business momentum in Southeast Asia.

title: Global Data Center Colocation Market to Reach USD 327.3 Billion by 2035, Expanding at a 14.5% CAGR: Custom Market Insights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth Rate, Value, SWOT Analysis)
author: Custom Market Insights
description: The global data center colocation market is projected to grow from USD 84.5 billion in 2025 to USD 327.3 billion by 2035, expanding at a 14.5% CAGR. Growth is driven by increasing demand for edge computing, low-latency applications, government digitalization initiatives, and data localization requirements. North America currently holds the largest market share, while Asia Pacific is expected to grow at the highest CAGR during the forecast period.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key market player in a rapidly growing industry with 14.5% CAGR and strong demand drivers from edge computing and digitalization initiatives.

title: Why One Real Estate Fund Dumped $62 Million of Cousins Properties Stock
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Resolution Capital sold 2.57 million shares (85% of its stake) in Cousins Properties during Q1 2026, valued at approximately $62.35 million. Despite the fund's exit, the article notes that Cousins Properties remains positioned as a potential office REIT winner due to its focus on premium Class A properties in high-growth Sun Belt markets, strong leasing pipeline, and solid balance sheet metrics.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While Resolution Capital significantly reduced its position (85% stake sale), the article presents a balanced view. The company faces headwinds from post-pandemic office sector challenges, but maintains competitive advantages through premium Sun Belt properties, strong leasing pipeline (1M sq ft), and the lowest leverage ratio among office REITs. Stock down 5% YTD underperforms S&P 500.
Listed as a top holding of Resolution Capital with $355.14 million (7.5% of AUM), indicating institutional confidence in the company.

title: What to Know About This Fund's $4 Million Exit From SmartStop Self Storage
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: GSI Capital Advisors fully exited its position in SmartStop Self Storage REIT (SMA) on May 14, 2026, selling 124,919 shares for approximately $4.01 million. Despite the exit, SmartStop's Q1 2026 fundamentals showed improvement with 20% revenue growth and a swing to profitability, though the stock has underperformed the S&P 500 by 38 percentage points over the past year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: GSI Capital's complete exit suggests loss of confidence in the specific investment, and the stock is down 10% YoY and underperforming the S&P 500 significantly. However, underlying business fundamentals improved with 20% revenue growth, swing to profitability, and management guidance for continued progress in 2026. The exit appears strategic reallocation rather than fundamental deterioration.
GSI Capital's largest holding at 11.4% of AUM ($21.0M), indicating strong confidence in the data center REIT as a core real estate investment.
Second-largest holding at 10.6% of AUM ($19.6M), demonstrating GSI Capital's continued confidence in the healthcare REIT sector.
